compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Context

A critical validation of the "behavioral and contractual test-coverage report" was
re-verified against `origin/main` (commit `6244666`; findings cross-checked at the
report's pinned `4e0c588`). The validation is mostly correct, with two notable results:

- The report's HIGH-priority claim that the concurrency-storm integration tests
  "self-disable via a non-strict xfail" is **INVALID** at both commits — the preflight
  is a functional `rebar init` probe (`tests/integration/test_store_concurrency.py`),
  not the "executable-under-repo-root" check the report describes, so a correct
  editable install gates CI normally. **No work is scheduled for that finding.**
- The remaining valid findings are genuine and are collected here. They cluster around
  compaction fault-atomicity, process-level durability contracts, a real
  (vs simulated) remote-append interleaving, input validation, docs drift, and
  test-suite hygiene.

Policy decisions resolved for this epic:
- **Whitespace-only ticket titles must be rejected** (current `if not title` in
  `src/rebar/_commands/composer.py` accepts them).

## Success Criteria

- Every valid finding from the validation is either fixed or has a scheduled child
  story, and each invalid finding is explicitly excluded (documented above).
- The compaction fold is provably all-or-none under forward- and reverse-rename
  failure, and recoverable after real process death.
- The async-push, remote-append, and parent-cascade contracts have tests that exercise
  the actual process/clone-level behavior, not in-process simulations.
- Stale docs (concurrency.md detached-HEAD claim, README `flock` framing,
  mutation-doc scope wording) are corrected.

## Acceptance Criteria

Each item below is a repo-observable end-state (verifiable by a command or artifact),
not a process milestone. This epic closes when every child is closed (rebar enforces the
open-children guard), i.e. `rebar deps <epic>` shows no open descendants.

- [ ] `src/rebar/_commands/compact.py` no longer unconditionally removes the snapshot in
      its rollback branch; a fault-injection test asserting snapshot retention on
      incomplete rollback exists and passes.
- [ ] `rebar create`/`edit` and the library/MCP create paths reject a whitespace-only
      title (a test passing `"   "` asserts an error) across CLI, library, and MCP.
- [ ] `docs/concurrency.md` references the detached-HEAD regression test,
      `README.md` describes the `fcntl`+`mkdir` lock (no external `flock`), and
      `docs/mutation-testing.md` scopes its scores to the five cores.
- [ ] New tests exist for: compaction rename fault injection, compactor process death,
      async-push parent-exit survival, real remote-append interleaving, and
      parent-cascade cross-clone races (grep-able test names present in the suite).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass on the merged result.
compiled_state.title: Test-coverage & correctness remediation (coverage-report validation)
